title: Butter Chicken
description: A rich and creamy chicken curry perfectly spiced and flavored with tomatoes.
steps: Marinate chicken with yogurt and spices for 2 hours.
Cook chicken pieces in a pan until lightly charred.
In a separate pan, melt butter and cook tomato purée with spices.
Add the cooked chicken and heavy cream. Simmer for 15 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve hot with naan.

title: Palak Paneer
description: Soft paneer cubes cooked in a smooth, vibrant, lightly spiced spinach curry.
steps: Blanch the spinach in boiling water, then blend it into a smooth purée.
Sauté onions, ginger, and garlic paste in a pan until golden.
Add chopped tomatoes and spices, cook until oil separates.
Pour in the spinach purée and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Add paneer cubes, mix gently, and serve hot.

title: Chole Bhature
description: A classic North Indian dish of spicy chickpea curry with fluffy fried bread.
steps: Pressure cook soaked chickpeas until tender.
Prepare a dough using flour, yogurt, and a pinch of baking soda. Let it rest.
Make a spicy gravy with onions, tomatoes, and chole masala.
Mix the cooked chickpeas into the gravy and simmer.
Roll dough into flat circles and deep fry to make puffy bhaturas.

title: Chicken Biryani
description: An aromatic rice dish cooked with marinated chicken, saffron, and traditional spices.
steps: Marinate chicken with yogurt and biryani spices.
Partially boil the basmati rice with whole spices.
In a heavy-bottomed pot, layer the marinated chicken and half-cooked rice.
Top with fried onions and drizzle saffron milk over the layers.
Seal the pot and cook on low heat (dum) for 30-40 minutes.

title: Masala Dosa
description: Crispy fermented crepe made from rice and lentils, filled with a spiced potato mixture.
steps: Mash the boiled potatoes.
Temper mustard seeds, curry leaves, and add onions. Cook until soft.
Add turmeric and the mashed potatoes to complete the filling.
Spread dosa batter thinly on a hot griddle and cook until crispy.
Place the potato filling in the center and fold.

title: Samosa
description: Crispy triangular pastries filled with a spicy potato and pea filling.
steps: Prepare a stiff dough using flour, oil, and water.
Mash boiled potatoes and mix with peas and spices.
Roll out circles of dough, cut them in half to form cones.
Fill cones with the potato mixture and seal the edges.
Deep fry until golden brown and crispy.

title: Dal Makhani
description: Slow-cooked black lentils and kidney beans finished with butter and cream.
steps: Soak lentils and beans overnight. Cook them until soft.
In a pan, cook tomato purée, ginger-garlic paste, and spices.
Mix the cooked lentils with the gravy.
Simmer on a very low flame for at least 1-2 hours.
Finish with generous amounts of butter and cream.

title: Pav Bhaji
description: A spicy mashed vegetable curry served with buttery toasted bread rolls.
steps: Boil and mash the mixed vegetables completely.
Sauté onions, tomatoes, and Pav Bhaji Masala in butter.
Add the mashed vegetables, season with salt, and simmer.
Slit the pav bread and toast it generously with butter on a griddle.
Serve hot with chopped onions and a wedge of lemon.

title: Aloo Gobi
description: A comforting dry curry of potatoes and cauliflower cooked with Indian spices.
steps: Heat oil in a pan and temper with cumin seeds.
Add potato cubes and cook until partially tender.
Add cauliflower florets, turmeric, salt, and other spices.
Cover and cook on low heat until vegetables are tender.
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.

title: Tandoori Chicken
description: Yogurt and spice-marinated chicken, traditionally roasted in an oven.
steps: Make deep cuts in the chicken pieces.
Marinate chicken with lemon juice, yogurt, and tandoori spices.
Let it rest in the refrigerator for at least 6 hours.
Grill or bake in a high-temperature oven until cooked and slightly charred.
Serve with mint chutney and onion rings.

title: Paneer Tikka
description: Grilled cubes of paneer and bell peppers pre-marinated in spiced yogurt.
steps: Mix yogurt, roasted gram flour, and spices to form a marinade.
Coat paneer and veggies well with the marinade.
Skewer alternating pieces of paneer, onion, and bell peppers.
Grill or pan-fry until charred and cooked on all sides.
Sprinkle chaat masala before serving.

title: Rogan Josh
description: A deeply flavored, aromatic lamb curry hailing from Kashmir.
steps: Brown the lamb pieces in oil/ghee with whole spices.
Add Kashmiri chili powder and a little water, cook on low heat.
Stir in fennel powder, ginger powder, and yogurt.
Cover and simmer gently for about 1-1.5 hours until the meat is incredibly tender.
Serve with steamed rice or naan.

title: Vada Pav
description: Mumbai's famous street food—a spicy potato dumpling inside a soft bun.
steps: Temper mustard seeds, curry leaves, and green chilies. Mix with mashed potatoes.
Form potato balls, dip in chickpea batter, and deep fry (Vada).
Slit the pav bun, spread green chutney and sweet chutney.
Sprinkle dry garlic chutney and place the hot vada in the center.
Press gently and savor.

title: Malai Kofta
description: Fried potato and paneer dumplings served in a rich, mild, slightly sweet gravy.
steps: Mix paneer, potato, and cornflour. Form balls stuffed with cashews.
Deep fry the stuffed balls (koftas) until golden and crisp.
Prepare a creamy gravy using onion-tomato purée and spices.
Cook the gravy thoroughly and add heavy cream.
Pour gravy over koftas just before serving.

title: Gajar Ka Halwa
description: A classic Indian dessert made from grated carrots, milk, sugar, and dry fruits.
steps: Sauté grated carrots in ghee for a few minutes.
Add milk and cook on low heat until the carrots are soft and milk is almost absorbed.
Stir in sugar and cook until the mixture dries up and releases ghee.
Add cardamom powder and mixed chopped nuts.
Serve warm.
